questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: One mole of a monoatomic gas is enclosed in a cylinder and occupies a volume of 4 liter at a pressure $100 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$. It is subjected to a process $\mathrm{T}=\alpha \mathrm{V}^2$, where $\alpha$ is a positive constant, V is volume of the gas and T is Kelvin temperature. Find the work-done by gas (in joule) in increasing the volume of gas to six times initial volume.
questions.solution:
$w=\int P d V ; P=\frac{n R T}{V} ; P=\alpha n R V \Rightarrow n \alpha R \int_{V_0}^{6 V_0} V d v=\frac{n \alpha R}{2}\left[\left(6 V_0\right)^2-\left(V_0\right)^2\right]$
$w=\frac{h \alpha R}{2}(35) V_0^2=\frac{P_0 V_0 \times 35}{2} ;\left(\because \alpha=\frac{P_0}{n R V_0}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An ideal gas undergoes a process shown in the P-V diagram. If the change in heat content of the gas in this process is $\frac{K}{2} P_0 V_0$. Then find the value of K :
questions.solution:
work done $=\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{P}_0 \mathrm{~V}_0$

$\Delta U=\frac{P_2 V_2-P_1 V_1}{\gamma-1}$
$\Delta Q=\frac{3}{2} P_0 V_0$

$\therefore \mathrm{k}=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A container having base area $\mathrm{A}_0$. Contains mercury upto a height $\ell_0$. At its bottom a thin tube of length $4 \ell_0$ and cross-section area $\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{A} \ll \mathrm{A}_0\right)$ having lower end closed is attached. Initially the length of mercury in tube is $3 \ell_0$. In remaining part 2 mole of a gas at temperature T is closed as shown in figure. Determine the work done (in joule) by gas if all mercury is displaced from tube by heating slowly the gas in the rear end of the tube by means of a heater. (Given : density of mercury $=\rho$, atmospheric pressure $\mathrm{P}_0=2 \ell_0 \rho \mathrm{~g}, \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{v}}$ of gas $=3 / 2 \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{A}=(3 /$ $\rho) \mathrm{m}^2, \ell_0=(1 / 9) \mathrm{m}$, all units in S.I. $)$
questions.solution:
If $x$ is length of mercury in tube then pressure of gas
$\mathrm{P}^{\prime}=\mathrm{P}_0+\rho \mathrm{g} \ell_0+\rho \mathrm{gx}=3 \rho g \ell_0+\rho g x$
$\mathrm{W}=-\int_{3 \ell_0}^0\left(3 \rho \mathrm{~g} \ell_0+\rho \mathrm{gx}\right) \mathrm{Adx}=13.5 \rho \mathrm{gA} \ell_0^2=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: One mole of ideal monoatomic gas is taken along a cyclic process as shown in the figure. Process $1 \rightarrow 2$ shown is $1 / 4^{\text {th }}$ part of a circle as shown by dotted line process $2 \rightarrow 3$ is isochoric while $3 \rightarrow 1$ is isobaric. If efficiency of the cycle is $n \%$ where $n$ is an integer. Find $n$.
questions.solution:
$W_{\text {net }}=\left(2 P_0 v_0\right)-\left(P_0 v_0\right)-\frac{\pi P_0 v_0}{4}$ $\mathrm{W}_{\text {net }}=\mathrm{P}_0 \mathrm{v}_0-\frac{\pi \mathrm{P}_0 \mathrm{v}_0}{4}=(4-\pi) \frac{\mathrm{P}_0 \mathrm{v}_0}{4} ;$ Put $\pi=3.14$ $W_{\text {net }}=\frac{0.86}{4} P_0 v_0=(0.22)\left(P_0 v_0\right)$
$\Delta Q_{1 \rightarrow 2}=(4.5)\left(P_0 v_0\right)+(1.22)\left(P_0 v_0\right)=(5.72)\left(P_0 v_0\right)$ $\Delta Q_{2 \rightarrow 3}=-3 P_0 v_0+0=-3\left(P_0 v_0\right)$ $\Delta Q_{3 \rightarrow 1}=-1.5\left(P_0 v_0\right)-\left(P_0 v_0\right)=-2.5\left(P_0 v_0\right)$ Thus efficiency $\eta=\frac{W_{\text {net }}}{+ \text { veheat }}$ $\eta=\frac{0.22\left(P_0 v_0\right)}{(5.72)\left(P_0 v_0\right)}=0.04$ Thus efficiency is $4 \%$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
